message:
feat: massive test expansion + storage/protocol/streaming improvements

Test coverage explosion across all backend modules and core subsystems:
- insanely_fast_native: +337 lines of edge-case tests (empty audio,
  concurrent sessions, malformed JSON recovery, timeout handling)
- backend/mod router: latency timing state tracking with
  update_router_state(), +169 lines of router dispatch tests
- native_audio: +361 lines testing sample rate conversion, channel
  mixing, buffer underrun, silence detection edge cases
- whisper_cpp_native: +372 lines covering model loading failures,
  streaming chunk boundaries, language detection confidence
- whisper_diarization_native: +567 lines for speaker overlap,
  cross-talk segmentation, voice activity detection thresholds
- speculation: window_to_seq HashMap for O(1) speculative lookup,
  +1160 lines testing speculative decoding window management
- streaming: config refactoring, +833 lines testing reconnection,
  backpressure, partial frame reassembly
- sync: overwritten_run_ids cascade-delete tracking for consistent
  cleanup, +841 lines testing concurrent sync conflict resolution

Storage improvements:
- New acceleration_json column for caching per-segment acceleration
  metadata without re-parsing
- Migration refactoring for cleaner schema evolution
- WAL-to-DELETE journal mode switching for safer multi-process access
- sql_ident() safe identifier quoting to prevent SQL injection in
  dynamic table/column names

Protocol/format changes:
- robot.rs: removed "replay" from required schema fields (replay is
  now optional for lightweight robot definitions); added
  ordering_contract metadata for deterministic replay sequencing
- tty_audio.rs: raw_pcm changed from Vec<u8> to BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u8>>
  for natural dedup and chronological ordering of PCM chunks;
  duplicate handshake now returns an error; exhaustive match on
  all message variants
- orchestrator: CTC forced alignment test fixture corrections

Integration/e2e tests updated:
- cli_integration: voiced WAV helper for realistic test audio
- e2e_pipeline: updated for new storage schema
- robot_contract: validates relaxed required-fields schema
- tty_control: exercises BTreeMap-based PCM ordering

New doc: docs/tty-replay-guarantees.md documenting the deterministic
replay contract and ordering invariants.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+# TTY Replay Guarantees
+
+This note defines operator-facing replay guarantees for TTY audio transport.
+It is a companion to `docs/tty-audio-protocol.md` and does not replace the
+wire-level protocol spec.
+
+## Scope
+
+Guarantees apply to protocol version `1` with NDJSON framing, using the
+existing control/data frame model.
+
+## Deterministic Framing Guarantees
+
+- Frame boundaries are line-oriented NDJSON: one JSON object per line.
+- Data frame sequencing is strict by `seq` (`u64`), starting at `0`.
+- Decoder behavior is deterministic for a fixed input stream and recovery mode:
+  - `fail_closed`: first violation terminates decode.
+  - `skip_missing`: violations are recorded, valid later frames continue.
+
+## Integrity Guarantees
+
+- Optional `crc32` and `payload_sha256` are interpreted over raw
+  pre-compression audio bytes.
+- Integrity failures are deterministic outcomes:
+  - `fail_closed`: hard failure.
+  - `skip_missing`: frame dropped and recorded in telemetry.
+
+## Replayability Guarantees
+
+- Given identical framed input and identical decode policy, decoded output bytes
+  are deterministic.
+- Retransmit planning is deterministic:
+  - requested sequences are sorted, deduplicated;
+  - contiguous sequences are collapsed into stable ranges.
+- `RetransmitLoop` behavior is deterministic for fixed inputs/loss pattern:
+  - stable round progression;
+  - stable recovery strategy escalation;
+  - stable report contents.
+
+## Event/Run-Log Coupling
+
+- TTY sequence/integrity outcomes should be persisted with run artifacts
+  (`events` + replay metadata) so decode/retransmit decisions are auditable.
+- Operator replay requires preserving:
+  - framed NDJSON stream,
+  - decode policy (`fail_closed` or `skip_missing`),
+  - retransmit plan/report artifacts.
+
+## Non-Guarantees
+
+- No claim of bit-identical compressed payloads across different encoders.
+- No claim of packet/network timing determinism; guarantees are content/order
+  based.
+- No automatic two-way merge of divergent retransmit sessions.
